reasoning: The Lean statement requires, for a normal space X and any pair of disjoint closed sets A, B, the existence of open sets U ⊇ A and V ⊇ B whose closures are disjoint. In Munkres, "neighborhood of a set" means an open set containing that set, so the use of `IsOpen U ∧ IsOpen V ∧ A ⊆ U ∧ B ⊆ V` faithfully captures "neighborhoods U of A and V of B." The condition `closure U ∩ closure V = ∅` is exactly "their closures are disjoint." The Mathlib `NormalSpace` class (disjoint closed sets have disjoint open neighborhoods) is the standard definition used in Munkres. Quantifier structure, types, and hypotheses all match the informal statement.
